title: Full course lifecycle happy path: intake through content generation
preconditions: A student account exists with valid credentials
The AI API is reachable and returns successful responses
No active course exists for the student
steps: Student authenticates with valid username and password
Student initiates a new course and the Chair agent begins intake questionnaire
Student answers all intake questions covering topic, knowledge level, objectives, and weekly availability
Chair agent presents a generated syllabus with point distribution across homework and final project
Student requests a modification to the syllabus and the Chair agent produces a revised version
Student explicitly approves the revised syllabus
Chair agent assigns homework due dates derived from the syllabus and presents the schedule
Student agrees to the due date schedule
System generates all lesson content and homework assignments upfront
expected_result: The course transitions to active status with all lesson content and homework assignments available. Due dates are stored as agreed. The student's dashboard reflects the new active course with the approved syllabus and point distribution.

title: Content generation does not proceed before student approves syllabus
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
Chair agent has completed intake and presented an initial syllabus
Student has not yet approved the syllabus
steps: Confirm the course status is pending syllabus approval
Attempt to access generated lesson content before approving the syllabus
Verify the system has not initiated or completed any content generation
expected_result: No lesson content or homework assignments exist for the course. The API returns a 404 or equivalent error indicating content is unavailable. Course status remains in syllabus-pending state.

title: Single active course enforcement: second course request rejected
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student already has one active course
steps: Student attempts to initiate a second course by sending a new course request
System evaluates the student's current active course count
expected_result: The system rejects the new course request with an error indicating the student already has an active course. No new course record is created. The existing active course is unaffected.

title: Single active course enforcement: course initiation succeeds after previous course is archived
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has one active course
steps: Student withdraws from the active course
Verify the course status changes to archived
Student initiates a new course request
Verify the Chair agent begins intake for the new course
expected_result: After archival of the first course, the system accepts the new course request and the Chair agent proceeds with intake. The archived course record is preserved with archived status.

title: Student data privacy: student cannot access another student's course data
preconditions: Student A and Student B both have accounts and active courses with lesson content
Student A is authenticated
steps: Student A retrieves their own course ID from the dashboard
Student A constructs a request for Student B's course data using Student B's course ID
Student A sends the request to the course content endpoint
Repeat for submissions, grades, and chat history endpoints
expected_result: All cross-student data requests are rejected with 403 Forbidden responses. Student A's authenticated session cannot retrieve any data belonging to Student B, including course content, submissions, grades, and chat history.

title: Course archival and resumption restores exact course state
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has an active course with two completed homework submissions and one section in progress
steps: Record the current course state: completed submissions, grades, current section, and due dates
Student withdraws from the course
Verify the course status is archived and the student has no active course
Student requests to resume the archived course
Verify the course status returns to active
Verify all prior submissions, grades, current section, and due dates are unchanged
expected_result: The resumed course matches the pre-archival state exactly. No submissions, grades, section progress, or due dates are lost or altered during the archive-resume cycle.

title: Late submission triggers five-percent-per-day penalty
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has an active course with a homework assignment whose due date is two calendar days in the past
The homework has not yet been submitted
steps: Student uploads a valid Markdown homework file for the overdue assignment
System accepts the submission and records the submission timestamp
Grading agent evaluates the submission content and assigns a raw score
System applies the late penalty for two days overdue
expected_result: The final recorded grade equals the raw score minus ten percent (two days multiplied by five percent per day). The grade record includes the raw score, penalty amount, days late, and final score as separate fields.

title: On-time submission receives no late penalty
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has an active course with a homework assignment whose due date is tomorrow
steps: Student uploads a valid AsciiDoc homework file before the due date
System accepts the submission
Grading agent produces a raw score
System calculates the final grade
expected_result: The final grade equals the raw score with zero penalty applied. The days-late field in the grade record is zero.

title: Badge awarded at milestone and final grade improvement applied
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has an active course configured with a badge milestone for scoring above 90 on three consecutive homework assignments
The student has scored above 90 on two consecutive assignments
steps: Student submits the third consecutive homework and receives a raw score above 90
System evaluates milestone criteria and determines the badge threshold is met
System awards the milestone badge to the student's account
System applies the configured final grade improvement associated with the badge
Student views their badge collection and updated grade projection
expected_result: The badge appears in the student's badge collection with the correct milestone description. The final grade projection reflects the improvement reward. The badge award and grade adjustment are recorded as separate events in the student's grade record.

title: Badge late-penalty waiver redeemed on a late submission
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student holds an unspent late-penalty waiver badge
The student has an active course with a homework assignment that is one calendar day overdue
steps: Student submits a valid LaTeX homework file for the overdue assignment
System records the submission as one day late
Grading agent assigns a raw score
Student redeems the late-penalty waiver badge against this submission
System recalculates the grade with the waiver applied
expected_result: The late penalty is fully waived. The final grade equals the raw score. The waiver badge is marked as spent and cannot be redeemed again. The grade record documents the waiver redemption event.

title: Spent waiver badge cannot be redeemed a second time
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has already redeemed their late-penalty waiver badge on a previous submission
steps: Student submits another late homework assignment
Student attempts to redeem the already-spent waiver badge
System evaluates the badge redemption request
expected_result: The system rejects the redemption with an error indicating the badge has already been used. The full late penalty is applied to the grade. The badge remains in spent state.

title: Account deletion safely terminates in-flight content generation
preconditions: A student account exists
Content generation for the student's course is currently in progress
An admin user is authenticated
steps: Verify the generation pipeline reports an in-progress status for the student's course
Admin submits an authorized deletion request for the student account
System signals termination to the in-flight generation operation
System waits for the generation operation to reach a safe stop point
System permanently deletes all personal data associated with the student account
expected_result: The generation operation terminates without partial writes or orphaned resources. All personal data including course records, submissions, grades, chat history, and authentication credentials is permanently deleted. No data belonging to the deleted account is retrievable afterward.

title: AI API exhausts retries: student notified and generation halted
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has approved a syllabus and content generation has begun
The AI API is configured to fail on all calls with a non-retryable error after exhausting the configured retry limit
steps: Content generation begins and the system issues an AI API call
The AI API returns an error; the system retries up to the configured limit
All retries are exhausted without a successful response
System halts the content generation pipeline
System sends a failure notification to the student
expected_result: Content generation stops completely with no partial or corrupted content written to the course. The student receives a notification describing the failure. The course status reflects a generation-failed state, not an active or completed state.

title: Content generation timeout triggers student notification
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has approved a syllabus and content generation has begun
The content generation timeout is configured to a short interval for testing
The AI API is responding slowly such that generation will not complete within the timeout
steps: Content generation begins after syllabus approval
The configurable time limit elapses before generation completes
System detects the timeout condition
System dispatches a timeout notification to the student
expected_result: The student receives a notification indicating content generation did not complete within the expected time limit. The notification is delivered while the course is still in a generation-in-progress state, allowing the student to contact support.

title: Admin audit trail records create, modify, and deactivate actions
preconditions: An admin account exists and is authenticated
The audit log is empty or scoped to this admin's session for verification
steps: Admin creates a new student account with username, email, and role
Admin modifies the student account by updating the email address
Admin deactivates the student account
Admin retrieves the audit log entries filtered by the target student account ID
expected_result: Three audit log entries exist for the target account: one for create, one for modify, and one for deactivate. Each entry records the admin's identity, the action type, a timestamp, and the before/after state of the affected fields. No entries are missing or duplicated.

title: Brute-force lockout: five consecutive failures lock the account
preconditions: A student account exists with a known username
The account is not currently locked
steps: Submit five consecutive login attempts with an incorrect password for the student account
Attempt a sixth login with the correct password
expected_result: After the fifth consecutive failure the account is locked. The sixth login attempt with the correct password is rejected with an account-locked error, not an invalid-credentials error. The lockout persists for the configured duration.

title: Brute-force lockout edge case: four failures then success resets counter
preconditions: A student account exists with known correct credentials
The account is not currently locked
steps: Submit four consecutive login attempts with an incorrect password
Submit one login attempt with the correct password
Verify the student is authenticated and the account is not locked
Submit five more consecutive login attempts with an incorrect password
expected_result: After four failures and one success the account remains unlocked and the failure counter resets to zero. The subsequent five failures then trigger a lockout, confirming the counter was correctly reset by the successful login.

title: Password reset flow: token delivered, used once, second use rejected
preconditions: A student account exists with a registered email address
The student is not authenticated
steps: Student submits a password reset request for their username
System delivers a reset token via the out-of-band channel to the registered email
Student submits the token along with a new password
System validates the token and updates the password
Student authenticates successfully with the new password
Student submits the same reset token a second time with another new password
expected_result: The first use of the reset token succeeds and the password is updated. The second use of the same token is rejected with a token-already-used or token-expired error. The password remains as set by the first successful reset.

title: Password reset rate limit: fourth request within one hour is rejected
preconditions: A student account exists
No password reset requests have been made in the current hour
steps: Submit three password reset requests for the account within the same hour
Submit a fourth password reset request for the same account within the same hour
expected_result: The first three reset requests are accepted and tokens are issued. The fourth request within the same hour is rejected with a rate-limit error. No token is issued for the fourth request.

title: CSRF protection: state-changing request without valid token is rejected
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated with an active session
steps: Construct a state-changing POST request to the homework submission endpoint
Send the request with a missing CSRF token
Construct the same request with a malformed CSRF token
Send the malformed-token request
expected_result: Both requests are rejected with 403 Forbidden responses. No submission is recorded. The student's session remains valid and unaffected by the rejected requests.

title: Per-student token cap: AI processing halts when limit is reached
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student's per-course AI token cap is configured to a low value for testing
The student's active course has consumed tokens up to within a small margin of the cap
steps: Student submits a chat message to the Chair agent that would require an AI API call
The AI API call would push total token expenditure past the configured per-course cap
System checks current token usage against the cap before issuing the API call
expected_result: The system halts AI processing without issuing the API call. The student receives an informative error indicating the token limit for this course has been reached. No additional AI API calls are made for this student's course until the cap is adjusted by an admin.

title: Section feedback triggers regeneration of that section's content
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has an active course with at least one fully generated section
The AI API is reachable and returns successful responses
steps: Student reaches the end of a section and submits feedback requesting a change in explanation depth
System records the feedback and signals the content-generation agent to regenerate the section
Agent regenerates the section content incorporating the student's feedback
Student retrieves the updated section content
expected_result: The section content is replaced with a newly generated version. The new content is different from the original and reflects the student's feedback. The course record shows the section was regenerated, and no other sections are affected.

title: Section feedback without change request does not trigger regeneration
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has an active course with at least one fully generated section
steps: Student submits positive feedback at the end of a section indicating satisfaction with the content
System records the feedback
Verify no regeneration job is queued or executed for the section
expected_result: The section content remains unchanged. No regeneration API call is issued. The feedback is recorded in the course record as a positive review without triggering any content pipeline activity.

title: Admin oversight: admin can view all courses and statuses system-wide
preconditions: Three student accounts exist with courses in different states: one active, one archived, one completed
An admin account exists and is authenticated
steps: Admin requests the system-wide course list
Admin filters the list by status to view only active courses
Admin filters the list by status to view only archived courses
Admin filters the list by status to view only completed courses
expected_result: The system-wide course list returns all courses across all students. Each filter returns only courses matching the requested status. Each course entry includes the student identity, course topic, current status, and last-modified timestamp.

title: File submission format validation: non-conforming file is rejected
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has an active course with an outstanding homework assignment
steps: Student uploads a file with a .pdf extension for the homework submission
Student uploads a file with a .docx extension for the homework submission
Student uploads a file with a .txt extension for the homework submission
expected_result: All three upload attempts are rejected with format-validation errors indicating only LaTeX, Markdown, and AsciiDoc formats are accepted. No submission record is created for any rejected file. The assignment remains outstanding.
